description: Issues Panel — Add GitHub Issues and GitLab Issues sections to the sidebar, reusing existing authentication and following the same patterns as Pull Requests and Merge Requests.

userStories.title: GitHub Issues IPC handlers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need main process IPC handlers for listing and fetching GitHub Issues.
acceptanceCriteria: New IPC handler github:listIssues in git-ipc.ts or index.ts accepts (owner: string, repo: string, state?: string) and returns array of issues
Each issue has: number, title, state, author, authorAvatar, labels (array of {name, color}), assignees (string[]), createdAt, updatedAt, commentCount
Uses getGitHubToken() for authentication (same helper as PR handlers)
Returns { success: false, error: 'Not logged in to GitHub' } when no token
GitHub API endpoint: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues?state={state}&per_page=50&sort=updated&direction=desc
CRITICAL: Filter out pull requests from results — GitHub issues endpoint includes PRs. Check that item.pull_request is undefined/absent before including
New IPC handler github:getIssue accepts (owner, repo, issueNumber) and returns full issue with body + comments
Comments fetched from GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{number}/comments?per_page=50
Preload exposes window.electronAPI.github.listIssues(owner, repo, state?) and window.electronAPI.github.getIssue(owner, repo, issueNumber)
Type definitions added to useLayoutState.ts window.electronAPI.github block
Typecheck passes, existing tests pass
userStories.notes: Follow the exact pattern of the existing github:listPullRequests and github:getPullRequest handlers. Use the same githubApiRequest helper. Label colors from GitHub are hex WITHOUT the # prefix — add # when mapping. The issues endpoint returns PRs too — filter them: if (item.pull_request) skip. Comment shape: { id, author: c.user?.login, authorAvatar: c.user?.avatar_url, body: c.body, createdAt: c.created_at }.

userStories.title: GitLab Issues IPC handlers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need main process IPC handlers for listing and fetching GitLab Issues.
acceptanceCriteria: New IPC handler gitlab:listIssues accepts (projectPath: string, state?: string, instanceUrl?: string) and returns array of issues
Each issue has: iid, title, state, author, authorAvatar, labels (array of {name, color}), assignees (string[]), createdAt, updatedAt, commentCount
Uses getGitLabConfig(instanceUrl) for authentication (same pattern as MR handlers)
GitLab API endpoint: GET /api/v4/projects/{encoded_path}/issues?state={state}&per_page=50&order_by=updated_at&sort=desc
State mapping: 'open' -> 'opened', 'closed' -> 'closed' (same as MR mapping)
New IPC handler gitlab:getIssue accepts (projectPath, issueIid, instanceUrl?) and returns full issue with body + comments (notes)
Comments from GET /api/v4/projects/{encoded_path}/issues/{iid}/notes?per_page=50&sort=asc, filter out system notes (n.system === true)
Preload exposes window.electronAPI.gitlab.listIssues(projectPath, state?, instanceUrl?) and window.electronAPI.gitlab.getIssue(projectPath, issueIid, instanceUrl?)
Type definitions added to useLayoutState.ts window.electronAPI.gitlab block
Typecheck passes, existing tests pass
userStories.notes: Follow the exact pattern of gitlab:listMergeRequests and gitlab:getMergeRequest handlers. Use gitlabApiRequest helper. GitLab label colors already include # prefix. The forInstanceUrl parameter pattern is established — use the same approach. State mapping is the same as MRs: open->opened, closed->closed.

userStories.title: GitHub Issues sidebar section
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see GitHub Issues in the sidebar so I can track issues alongside my code.
acceptanceCriteria: New GitHubIssuesSection component in Sidebar.tsx, rendered below PullRequestsSection
Shows 'Log in to GitHub in Settings to view issues' when not logged in
Shows 'Not a GitHub repository' when repo has no GitHub remote
Reuses ghInfo (owner/repo) from the existing GitHub remote parsing in the sidebar — do NOT re-parse, share the state or compute it the same way
Open/Closed filter tabs with same styling as PR section (styles.prFilterBtn, styles.prFilterBtnActive)
Each issue row shows: #number, title, colored label badges, comment count icon (MessageSquare from lucide) if commentCount > 0

Clicking an issue opens a detail overlay (reuse styles.prDetailOverlay pattern) showing: title, #number, state badge (open=green, closed=red), author with avatar, labels, body as pre-formatted text, comments list
Detail overlay has 'Open in GitHub' button that opens the issue URL in browser
Detail overlay has close button (X) and backdrop click to dismiss
Refresh button in section header
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Copy the structure of PullRequestsSection almost exactly. Replace PR-specific fields (headBranch, baseBranch, mergeable) with issue-specific fields (labels, assignees). The detail overlay pattern is: {(selectedIssue || detailLoading) && (<div className={styles.prDetailOverlay} onClick={dismiss}>...)}. Reuse the same CSS classes — prDetailOverlay, prItem, prItemSelected, prFilterBtn, etc. For label badges, render as small colored spans with background-color from the label's color field.

userStories.title: GitLab Issues sidebar section
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see GitLab Issues in the sidebar for GitLab-hosted repos.
acceptanceCriteria: New GitLabIssuesSection component in Sidebar.tsx, rendered below MergeRequestsSection
Shows 'Log in to GitLab in Settings to view issues' when not logged in
Shows 'Not a GitLab repository' when repo has no GitLab remote
Reuses glInfo (projectPath, webUrl, instanceUrl) from the existing GitLab remote parsing
Open/Closed filter tabs matching MR section style
Each issue row shows: #iid, title, colored label badges, comment count
Clicking an issue opens detail overlay with title, state, author, labels, body, comments
Detail overlay has 'Open in GitLab' button
Passes instanceUrl through to all IPC calls
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Copy the structure of MergeRequestsSection. Same pattern as US-IS-003 but for GitLab. The glInfo state is already computed by the MR section — share it or compute identically. GitLab issue URLs: {webUrl}/-/issues/{iid}.
